Observation: When the proof can compute a rigid local invariant and oppose it to a global requirement, the argument closes or nearly closes.
Testable implication: Larger corpora should show higher completion rates when a rigid local invariant is available.

Observation: Long chains of overdetermined rigidity lemmas produce the most robust completions in the pilot.
Testable implication: Proofs dominated by rigidity lemmas should have the highest generalization-robustness rates.

Observation: Explicit route elimination is not wasted effort: it permanently shrinks search and improves downstream focus.
Testable implication: Route-elimination counts should correlate positively with eventual completion or with cleaner remaining gaps.

Observation: Turning analytic conditions into discrete or finite targets opens productive search moves.
Testable implication: Steps that reduce infinite or continuous conditions to finite combinatorial ones should predict accepted downstream steps.

Observation: The model can often close a small case, but the mechanism fails to transport to the full problem.
Testable implication: Early local wins with low generalization robustness should predict non-completion.

Observation: Some accepted steps solve a local obligation yet contribute no mechanism that survives to the real target.
Testable implication: Accepted steps with goal completion = 1 and generalization robustness = 0 should cluster in structurally hard problems.

Observation: The two outright invalid inferences occur while extending a locally plausible statement beyond its admissible scope.
Testable implication: In larger corpora, invalid steps should cluster at generalization moves rather than at base cases.

Observation: A derivation can be valid conditional on a premise that was never established or cited.
Testable implication: Premises described as standard without evidence should show elevated partial-step rates.

Pattern: Generalization robustness separates complete from partial proofs
Observation: Assumption compliance and inference validity are near ceiling across the pilot. Most of the meaningful variation sits in generalization robustness.
Testable implication: Mid-proof generalization-robustness rate should predict completion better than inference-validity rate.

Pattern: Self-rejection is better at route screening than derivation auditing
Observation: The model correctly rejects many dead routes, but misses some invalid inferences that only the audit catches.
Testable implication: The self-rejected to audit-rejected ratio should stay above 1 if route screening is easier than derivation auditing.

Observation: A single counterexample or obstruction can remove an entire proof family.
Testable implication: Elimination steps should deliver the highest uncertainty reduction per unit compute.

Pattern: Positive and negative discovery remain balanced
Observation: The pilot produces both constructions and boundary information. Negative results are not leftovers; they are part of the usable output.
Testable implication: Larger corpora should preserve a roughly balanced positive-versus-negative discovery mix.

Pattern: Late steps become forced in complete proofs
Observation: Complete proofs narrow to one viable continuation, while incomplete proofs end with multiple live routes.
Testable implication: Late-stage branch width should discriminate complete from partial runs.

Pattern: Single-gap conditionals are closer to repair than strategy failures
Observation: A nearly complete chain with one explicit missing premise is structurally closer to closure than a run with several unresolved mechanisms.
Testable implication: One-gap conditionals should convert to complete proofs at higher repair rates than strategy-failure partials.
